task.prompt: A Spring Boot service class has two public methods: `placeOrder()` and `chargeCard()`. Both are annotated with `@Transactional`. When `placeOrder()` calls `chargeCard()` directly using `this.chargeCard()`, the transaction on `chargeCard()` is silently ignored — it runs inside the existing `placeOrder()` transaction without any isolation, even when `chargeCard()` is annotated with `@Transactional(propagation = Propagation.REQUIRES_NEW)`. Investigate why the `REQUIRES_NEW` propagation is not honoured and why the inner transaction never opens. The fix requires understanding how Spring AOP proxies work, how `@EnableTransactionManagement` registers interceptors, and why self-invocation bypasses the proxy.

hint_levels.symptom_only: When a Spring service method annotated with @Transactional(propagation = Propagation.REQUIRES_NEW) is called from within the same class via 'this.method()', the new transaction is never created. Database operations in the inner method execute in the outer transaction, causing rollback behaviour that is inconsistent with the declared propagation.
hint_levels.file_hint: The bug is architectural: Spring transaction management is implemented via AOP proxies generated by TransactionInterceptor (spring-tx). When a bean calls its own method through 'this', it bypasses the proxy entirely. The fix involves either (a) injecting the bean into itself via @Autowired or ApplicationContext, or (b) extracting chargeCard() into a separate @Service bean so the proxy is always invoked. Look at TransactionInterceptor.java and AbstractFallbackTransactionAttributeSource.java.
hint_levels.function_hint: In Spring AOP, only external calls enter the proxy chain. In TransactionInterceptor.invoke(), the interceptor is only reached when the call enters through the proxy reference — not through 'this'. In AbstractFallbackTransactionAttributeSource.computeTransactionAttribute() the method lookup works correctly, but it is never called for self-invocations. The standard fix: inject a self-reference (@Autowired private OrderService self) and call self.chargeCard(), or move chargeCard() to an injected dependency bean.

reasoning_chain: Read the symptom: REQUIRES_NEW transaction is not created on self-invocation
Open OrderService.java — see placeOrder() calling this.chargeCard() with REQUIRES_NEW
Open AopAutoConfiguration.java — confirm @EnableAspectJAutoProxy is set with proxyTargetClass=true
Open TransactionAutoConfiguration.java — confirm TransactionInterceptor is registered as an advice
Understand that Spring AOP wraps beans in a CGLIB proxy; the 'this' reference inside the bean points to the raw class, not the proxy
Open AbstractFallbackTransactionAttributeSource.java — trace computeTransactionAttribute() to verify lookup works but is never reached via self-call
Check TransactionInterceptor.invoke() — only fires when invoked through the proxy reference
Realize: self-invocation bypasses the proxy entirely, so REQUIRES_NEW is ignored silently
Apply fix: inject self-reference (@Autowired OrderService self) and call self.chargeCard() — the proxy is now invoked, REQUIRES_NEW opens a new transaction
